channel.title: physics.pop-ph updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: physics.pop-ph updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Do We Perceive Reality?
item.description: arXiv:2301.01204v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: The cognitive scientist Donald Hoffman argues that we don't perceive reality: spacetime, objects, colors, sounds, tastes, and so forth, are all merely an interface that we evolved to track evolutionary fitness rather than to perceive truths about external reality. In this paper, I expound on his argument, then I extend it, primarily, by looking at key ideas in physics that are quite germane to it. Among the topics in physics that I discuss are black holes, the holographic principle, string theory, duality, quantum gravity, and special relativity. I discuss these ideas from physics with an eye to their relevance for Hoffman's view.
item.dc:creator: John Klasios
